title: Comparison of Uncertainty Visualization Formats for Climate Change Adaptation
author: Müller, Laura
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.title: The Python script "OpenSource_uncertaintyvis_Python_3_11" outputs a...
steps.instruction: The Python script "OpenSource_uncertaintyvis_Python_3_11" outputs a figure showing and thus enabling comparison of five different uncertainty visualization formats for the same data. For this, it reads in data, for which the path to the data has to be put in. Then, it plots five different uncertainty visualization formats in one figure for the same data to compare the uncertainty visualization formats, which will be published in Müller and Döll (2023, preprint). The five uncertainty visualization formats used are

steps.title: GERICS bar chart (used e
steps.instruction: GERICS bar chart (used e.g. for graphs in the fact sheets from GERICS, see https://www.climate-service-center.de/products_and_publications/fact_sheets/landkreise/index.php.de),

steps.title: Letter-value plot (Hofmann, 2011)
steps.instruction: Letter-value plot (Hofmann, 2011). To test the script, the dataset "OpenSource_uncertaintyvis_df.csv" is published together with the Python script. The underlying data of the dataset was retrieved from the ISIMIP project, following the ISIMIP2b simulation protocol (ISIMIP, 2019), and can be accessed at https://www.isimip.org (last access: 27th of October

steps.title: (Frieler et al
steps.instruction: (Frieler et al., 2017). The data was processed and analyzed with Python 3.11 (https://www.python.org/) in the Python integrated development environment PyCharm (available with a license at https://www.jetbrains.com/pycharm/) as described in Müller and Döll (2023, preprint). The dataset contains 32 multi-model ensemble results of the variables "qr_w_26" and "qr_w_85", which are the simulated, mean change of groundwater recharge in the period 2070-2099 as compared to the reference period 1971-2000 for the winter months November, December, and January for the RCP scenarios 2.6 and 8.5 for the area of the UNESCO biosphere reserve Rhön (for more details see Müller and Döll, 2023, preprint). We would like to thank the ISIMIP project (www.isimip.org) for providing the model data. Frieler, K., Lange, S., Piontek, F., Reyer, C. P., Schewe, J., Warszawski, L., et al. (2017). Assessing the impacts of 1.5 °C global warming-simulation protocol of the Inter-Sectoral Impact Model Intercomparison Project (ISIMIP2b). Geoscientific Model Development, 10(12): 4321-4345, https://doi.org/10.5194/gmd-10-4321-
